name: Scenario C — The Contradiction Test
pitch: Three weeks ago I told you we use tabs. Two weeks ago, spaces. Last week, tabs again. New file — which do you use?
tests: Decay-weighted recency, contradiction handling, primacy/recency ordering (CoALA Phase 0 + Tier-B §10.2 + §10.4).

memories.title: Use tabs (v1)
memories.body: kestrel-api uses tab indentation. Configured editorconfig accordingly.
memories.content:
# Indentation: tabs

We agreed on tab indentation for kestrel-api. The editorconfig is updated.

memories.title: Switched to spaces (v2)
memories.body: Reversed prior decision — kestrel-api now uses 2-space indentation. Easier diff review with the JS ecosystem default.
memories.content:
# Indentation: spaces

Switched kestrel-api to 2-space indentation. Easier code-review on Github. Updated editorconfig.

memories.title: Back to tabs (v3 — final)
memories.body: Reverted again. kestrel-api uses tabs. Final this time — TC39 plus our biggest contributor prefers tabs. .editorconfig pinned to indent_style=tab, indent_size=4.
memories.content:
# Indentation: tabs (final)

Reverted from 2-space to tabs after team vote on 2026-05-21. Our heaviest contributor argued tabs are accessible to vision-impaired devs (configurable display width). This decision is final.

.editorconfig:
```
indent_style = tab
indent_size = 4
```

test.label: create-route-file
test.text: Create a new file src/routes/health.js in kestrel-api. It should export an Express route handler that responds with JSON `{ status: 'ok', uptime: <process uptime in seconds> }` for GET /health. Use our project's current indentation style — match the rest of the codebase.

judge.question: Create src/routes/health.js in kestrel-api following the project's current indentation style.
judge.oracle_answer: The file uses TAB indentation (not spaces). The project's final indentation decision (effective 2026-05-21) is tabs, indent_size=4. The handler is a minimal Express route returning { status: 'ok', uptime: <number> } on GET /health.
rubric: Indentation in the generated file uses TAB characters, not spaces
File is created at src/routes/health.js (exact path)
Exports an Express route or handler (router.get('/health', ...) or app.get equivalent)
Returns JSON containing 'uptime' from process.uptime() (or equivalent)
No conflicting comments like '// using spaces because…' or stale style notes
